text:
A was a serum-based method of turning a Subject of Ymir into a Titan.

Uses

The injection only worked on Subjects of Ymir and had no effect on other living things. While the exact chemical composition of Titan serum is unknown, it was considered by the Marleyans to be Titan spinal fluid. It has been shown that the serums quickly evaporated after coming into contact with air; for this reason, they were always kept in tightly-sealed vials. Since Titan corpses also quickly evaporated, it would make sense that their spinal fluid would do the same.

Rod Reiss had with him a "strongest" and an "armor" serum along with the regular serum. It is unknown if there were more types. The "powerful" serum, when ingested orally, turned Rod into a 120m Titan, which Kenny Ackermann theorized that he did not follow the process correctly, and the "armor" serum gave Eren Jaeger's Titan hardening abilities.

Titan serums were injected into the bloodstream, typically the inner forearm or the back of the neck; it could also be ingested. Very shortly after it was introduced into the bloodstream, the person generated a Titan body and became a "Pure Titan." Pure Titans were mindless until they consumed a human with the power of the Titans. Humans with the power of the Titans could take the serum again, and specialized serums could give the Nine Titans abilities they never had; it is unknown what would happen if they took the normal serum. After witnessing Rod Reiss turning into a disfigured 120m Titan when he licked the "strongest" serum, Kenny speculated that the method in which the serum entered the body matters, and if done improperly, there may be consequences. The size of the resulting Titan also appeared to be controllable, as Sergeant Major Gross claimed that he could turn an Eldian captive into a three- or four-meter Titan, and then successfully did it.

Marley's Public Security used the injection as a life sentence against Eldians who broke the law, including the Eldian Restorationists, to roam Paradis Island as Pure Titans for the rest of eternity as they ate other Eldians.

The Scout Regiment had used it as a means to save the lethally wounded by turning that person into a Titan, then having it devour another with the power of the Titans to restore them to human form. This was seen when Levi Ackermann used the serum to resurrect Armin Arlelt so he could eat Bertholdt Hoover and take the power of the Colossal Titan.

History

The origin of Titan injections and their usage is unknown, but it is probable that this was the method of creating new Titan monarchs within the Reiss family since the time of Karl Fritz. While unseen it is presumed the Tybur family possessed Titan injection's in order to pass down the War Hammer Titan every thirteen years within the family line. As well as by Marley which used a similar practice to the Reiss family in which members of the Warriors would inherit the Power of the Titans in a succession ritual.

Around 60 years before the fall of Wall Maria, Ymir and her cult of Ymir-worshipers were discovered by the Marley Public Security, and the man who gave "Ymir" her name claimed she had deceived him. Ymir chose to abide by her given name hoping to save the other members of the cult, but this proved fruitless and she was arrested with the other cultists. After being paraded around town and stoned by angry citizens, the cultists were sent to Paradis Island. Despite Ymir's pleas for mercy, she and the other cultists were transformed into Titans by the use of an injection.

In the year 832, a rebellious group of Eldians in Marley, the Eldian Restorationists, was discovered by the Marley Public Security, thanks to Zeke Jaeger. They were rounded up, sent to Paradis Island, and were turned into Pure Titans; however, one of the Marleyan officers was revealed to be Eren Kruger, "the Owl," and saved Grisha Jaeger from this fate. After giving Grisha a mission to reclaim the Founding Titan, Kruger injected him with the serum so Grisha can eat him and inherit the power of the Attack Titan and reach the Walls.

The Reiss family used the Titan injection to pass down the power of the Founding Titan through the generations. This practice had existed since the raising of the Walls. It saw Uri Reiss inherit the power from his father and, in 842, Frieda Reiss inherit it from him in turn.

Story

The Fall of Shiganshina arc

After the fall of Wall Maria, Grisha Jaeger traveled to the Reiss Chapel to speak with Queen Frieda. After battling with Frieda in her Titan form and slaughtering most of the Reiss family, he reunited with his son, Eren, and injected him with a serum, transforming him into a Titan who then devoured Grisha.

The Struggle for Trost arc

When cornered by the Trost District Garrison concerning his newfound Titan power, Eren suddenly recalls the moment when his father met him in the forest after the fall of Shiganshina. Insisting that Eren must return to the basement of their home, Grisha gave Eren an injection which he said would damage his memory and grant him a new ability. Furthermore, his father claimed that "their memories" would help Eren in learning how to use his new power.

Royal Government arc

Five years later in the Underground Chapel, Rod Reiss prepares to use an injection on his illegitimate daughter Historia. Upon seeing the syringe for the Titan injection, Eren begins wildly struggling against his chains, recognizing it as the kind his father had given him five years ago. Rod attempts to influence Historia into taking the injection and devouring Eren, however, after much contemplation, Historia rejects this plan and shatters the syringe. As she begins freeing Eren from his chains, Rod desperately licks the exposed serum, becoming a monstrous Titan. In order to save his comrades from the collapse of the Chapel's caverns, Eren bites into an injection vial labeled "Armor," granting him the ability to harden his Titan.

Levi later finds a heavily wounded Kenny Ackermann leaning against a tree and observes the man's injuries have left him on the verge of death. Kenny responds by smugly revealing that he has a Titan injection, which could allow him to save himself. However, Kenny does not use it on himself. As a final gift, Kenny decides to give Levi the Titan injection.

Dhalis Zachary meets with Dot Pyxis, Erwin Smith, and two of his Scouts to discuss the syringe left behind by Kenny Ackermann. Hange Zoë explains that its contents seem to originate from some sort of human bodily fluid, but it evaporates when exposed to open air, making it difficult to study. Hange suspects that whoever made this had access to a higher level of technology than they do. Since they cannot tamper it without ruining it, Pyxis suggests they use it for their current objective, and the syringe is entrusted to Levi Ackermann.

Return to Shiganshina arc

The injection given to Levi later becomes a subject of conflict as both Armin Arlelt and Erwin Smith are brought to him on the brink of death. Ultimately, Levi chooses to save Armin with the injection, and Armin's Pure Titan consumes Bertholdt Hoover, the Colossal Titan, and Armin inherits the Power of the Titans.

Marley arc

In 854, the Anti-Marleyan Volunteers present a large stock of Titan serum to the Garrison, explaining they had stolen it from Marley. However, Yelena admits duplicating it will be hard since they were unable to obtain any of the materials, equipment, or experts in Titan science they need. Dot Pyxis thanks the volunteers, while he begrudgingly arrests them over uncertainties regarding their leader.
